content:
8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

On July 16, Moonshot AI shipped Kimi K3 — a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model — and within 24 hours it did something no Chinese model had ever done: it took the #1 spot on Arena.ai’s Frontend Code Arena with an Elo of 1,679, ahead of Claude Fable 5 (1,631) and GPT-5.6 Sol (1,618). The two most advanced closed models on Earth, beaten at frontend coding by a model whose weights are promised for public download by July 27.
Then I dug into the Artificial Analysis report and found the number nobody is putting in their headlines: K3’s hallucination rate jumped from 39% to 51% compared with its predecessor. It answers more, and it fabricates more.
I spent the last two days reading every benchmark table, launch doc, and hands-on report I could find, and running K3 against the three models Moonshot itself called out: GPT-5.6 Sol, Claude Opus 4.8, and Claude Fable 5. Here’s the full picture — where K3 legitimately wins, where the launch claims fall apart, and exactly how to try it yourself in five minutes.
The claim that launched a thousand replies
Moonshot’s launch post says K3 “ranks second only to Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol.”
